DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-17-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs SHREE JALARAM MOTEL, L.P., SHREE SHIVA MURUGA, L.P., and SHREE JALARAM GENERAL PARTNERSHIP's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT:

Defendants Shree Jalaram Motel, LP, Shree Shiva Muruga LP, Shree Jalaram General Partnership, The Greenwich Inn, Motel Capri, Balwantsinh Thakor, Bhaveshsihn Thakor, Kiransinh Thakor, and Lataben Thakor's demurrer to Plaintiffs' Maria Elena Melgoza and Elder Chocoj's complaint is overruled as to all ten causes of action. Plaintiffs make sufficient allegations to support each cause of action and to lay out their theory for how each of the nine named defendants allegedly played a role in the Labor Code violations asserted in the complaint. (See, e.g., Complaint 1-14, 16-19, 29-30, 40, 51, 54-55, 59-60, 63-64, 68-69, 75, 77, 82, 84, 89, 97-99, 104, 111, 116.) Specifically, pay discrimination based on race is sufficiently alleged at paragraphs 30, 40, and 104 of the Complaint in support of the Equal Pay Act claim. In addition, Plaintiffs are entitled to name defendants under their fictitious business names so long as they amend their complaint to add the true corporate names upon discovery of the proper identities of the defendants. (Code Civ. Proc. 474 ["When the plaintiff is ignorant of the name of a defendant, he must state that fact in the complaint . . . and such defendant may be designated in any pleading or proceeding by any name, and when his true name is discovered, the pleading or proceeding must be amended accordingly"]; see also Pinkerton's Inc. v. Superior Court (1996) 49 Cal.App.4th 1342, 1349 [describing plaintiffs' actions in suing a defendant under a fictitious business name as "an entirely acceptable procedure"].)
Finally, Defendants' demurrer to the prayers for injunctive relief and punitive damages are procedurally improper. A demurrer may only be used to challenge the entirety of a complaint or individual causes of action, not prayers for relief. (Code Civ. Proc. 430.10; Venice Town Council, Inc. v. City of Los Angeles (1996) 47 Cal.App.4th 1547, 1562 ["[A] demurrer tests the sufficiency of the factual allegations of the complaint rather than the relief suggested in the prayer of the complaint."].)

Counsel for plaintiffs to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Howard Olsen, Esq., Law Offices of Michael Heath, Telephone 415-931-4207, appearing for Defendant(s) Shree Jalaram Motel, L.P. et al;

RULING - A R G U E D. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT BALWANTSINH THAKOR'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND.

Defendant Balwantsinh Thakor's motion to vacate the default entered on February 7, 2019 is granted. The motion is timely as it was filed approximately two months after the default was entered. The defendant provides a proposed pleading. Defendant demonstrates excusable neglect, mistake, and/or inadvertence caused entry of the default. Defendant's counsel was experiencing family issues during the time defendant's response was due. "[T]he policy of the law is to have every litigated case tried upon its merits, and it looks with disfavor upon a party, who, regardless of the merits of the case, attempts to take advantage of the mistake, surprise, inadvertence, or neglect of his adversary." (Weitz v. Yankosky (1966) 63 Cal.2d 849, 855; see also Fasuyi v. Permatex, Inc. (2008) 167 Cal.App.4th 681, 701.) Defendant shall file its response by May 21, 2019.

Order is signed.
